response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack web developer with strong TypeScript/Node.js experience and some systems-level work (low-latency DLang services, C/C++ IDE components), but the role demands SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear anywhere in this resume. His career trajectory is web platforms, mobile apps, and developer tooling, not silicon performance architecture, so the gap is fundamental rather than trainable, despite meeting the raw years-of-experience bar.
strengths: Deep performance-minded systems work at Sociomantic: low-latency DLang services handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O, fibers, and caching
Broad, adaptable engineering background spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with some C/C++ experience at National Instruments
Strong craftsmanship signals: open-source authorship, TDD advocacy, testing frameworks, and teaching/workshop experience
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or system-on-chip architecture experience whatsoever
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD accelerator background — the core of the role
Career is centered on web/application development, far from principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI/ML workload or GPU performance analysis background
Genuine low-latency systems performance work in DLang/C++
